source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A 3-phase, 440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.

The motor is coupled to a 220 V, separately excited D.C. generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \). Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted such that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded:

\( W_1 = 1800 W \), \( W_2 = -200 W \).

The speed of rotation of the stator magnetic field with respect to the rotor structure will be:
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: 1500 rpm in the opposite direction of rotation

choice_map.A: 90 rpm in the direction of rotation
choice_map.B: 90 rpm in the opposite direction of rotation
choice_map.C: 1500 rpm in the direction of rotation
choice_map.D: 1500 rpm in the opposite direction of rotation

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
multi_step: problem combines induction motor data, rotor-side feeding/stator resistance setup, DC generator load, wattmeter readings, and speed information
solving likely requires multiple linked steps: machine-speed relation plus interpretation of the electrical setup shown in the figure
multiple numerical givens are provided (440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole, 1410 rpm, W1, W2, generator/load values), indicating nontrivial reasoning rather than direct lookup
multiple-choice format with directional and speed alternatives suggests intermediate reasoning is needed to disambiguate options
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the expected answer is a specific speed and direction statement
choices provide a closed set of 4 candidate outputs, making correctness easy to check
machine parameters and measured values are explicit, so a downstream checker can verify the derivation against standard formulas
